name: Honey-Date Bars Low-Fat (d)
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Unbleached white flour or whole-wheat pastry flour
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Lightly oil a 9-inch square baking pan.
3. Filling: In a saucepan over medium heat, combine dates, raisins, and water. Simmer for 10 minutes or until the fruit is very soft. Drain the fruit (save the poaching liquid) and puree it with honey in a blender or food processor, adding reserved liquid as needed to achieve a spreadable consistency. Set aside.
4. Crust: In a large bowl, combine oats, flour, baking powder, cinnamon, ginger, nutmeg, and salt if desired. In another bowl, mix together honey, oil, applesauce, and vanilla. Add this mixture to the dry ingredients and stir until well blended.
5. Divide the dough into two parts. Press half of it into the prepared baking pan to form a crust. Spread the fruit filling over the crust using a spatula, making sure to spread to the corners of the pan. Sprinkle the remaining dough over the filling, again ensuring coverage at the corners. Gently pat the topping smooth.
6. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes or until golden brown. Allow to cool completely before cutting into bars (1 1/2 x 3 inches). Makes 18 bars.
Helpful Hint: Store bars in a tightly lidded container to prevent sogginess. They also freeze well.
